RollPrompt.Title: Roll: {label}
RollPrompt.InvalidDiceModifier: Enter a dice modifier like +1D, -2D, or +2.
RollPrompt.BelowOneD: This roll has been reduced below 1D and cannot be rolled.

Combat.NoAttackCode: No matching skill or attribute was found for {weapon}. Enter a skill or attribute name in Skill Used.
Combat.BelowOneD: This action has been reduced below 1D and cannot be rolled.
Combat.AmbiguousNote: The stun-setting equal-strength edge case is not resolved automatically. Check the rulebook entry before changing status.
Combat.RulesNote: Weapon, skill, attribute, Force, and resistance rolls now apply wound penalties, Dexterity armor penalties, and SW1E combat action penalties. Reaction uses still require GM judgment and manual tracker adjustment when they are not rolled from a reaction skill button.

Blocked.stunned: This character is stunned and cannot act until the wound status is cleared.
Blocked.incapacitated: This character is incapacitated and cannot take action rolls.
Blocked.mortallyWounded: This character is mortally wounded and cannot take action rolls.
Blocked.dead: This character is dead and cannot take action rolls.

ResistPrompt.Title: Damage Resistance: {actor}
ResistPrompt.ApplyStatus: Apply the resulting wound status automatically

Combat.NoActionCode: No matching skill or attribute was found for {label}.
Combat.ActionPenaltyLabel: Combat actions: -{dice} ({actions} declared, {reactions} reaction uses, segment {segment})
Combat.ActionStateLabel: Combat actions: {actions} declared, {reactions} reaction uses, segment {segment}

Tracker.OrderLocked: SW1E combat uses tracker order and action segments instead of a stock initiative roll. The current tracker order has been locked.
Tracker.Note: Set declared actions for the round on each combatant. Use the combat tracker Next Turn control to advance through each action segment.

Starship.Note: Set fire segments for each ship. Use the combat tracker Next Turn control to advance through piloting, speed, and fire phases.

Combat.NoTargetAutomation: No single target was selected, so damage and resistance were not resolved automatically.
Combat.InvalidWeaponDamage: This weapon does not have a valid damage code, so damage was not resolved automatically.

Grenade.Title: Grenade Attack: {weapon}
Grenade.PlaceTemplate: Click the map to place the {weapon} blast center. Right-click or press Escape to cancel.
Grenade.OutOfRange: That point is beyond the weapon's listed throw range.
Grenade.NoSourceToken: A grenade attack needs a placed source token on the scene.
Grenade.InvalidProfile: This grenade or detonator is missing blast data.
Grenade.NoTargetsInBlast: No actors were found inside the blast radius.
Grenade.DodgedBlast: Successful dodge against grenade; falls prone and avoids blast
Grenade.BlockedByWall: Blocked by wall; no grenade effect

Combat.LightsaberParryNote: Sense is used for this lightsaber parry. The stored defense applies against melee attacks and blaster bolts this segment.

Force.Title: Force Skills
Force.Untrained: {skill} is untrained and cannot be used.
Force.DarkSideWarning: This power carries an immediate Dark Side warning in the rule text.
Force.ManualResolution: At least one required target number was left at 0. The rolls were made, but final success must be adjudicated manually.
Force.NoRequiredSkills: This Force power does not have any required Force skills marked.
Force.RulesNote: Force support automates the three Force skills, Dark Side bonuses, multi-skill penalties, Force Point spending, and Force power activation when all required skills are attempted in the same round. Staged multi-round activations, perception/control resistance rolls, and proximity/relationship modifiers still need GM input.

RollPrompt.Title: Force Skill: {skill}
RollPrompt.KeepUp: Mark this power as kept up if successful

LightsaberParry.Title: Lightsaber Parry — {weapon}
LightsaberParry.NoLightsaber: This actor does not have a lightsaber available for Sense-based parries.

Builder.Title: Character Builder
Builder.OpenHint: Create a new hero from a Chapter One template.
Builder.ChapterRuleNote: Chapter One character generation starts from a template, allocates 7D across skills, allows no more than +2D in any one skill, and only lets you improve Force skills that are printed on the template.
Builder.CoreSkillListNote: This builder uses the printed template skill list from the character template cards, including the template-specific weapon skill exceptions.
Builder.OverspentWarning: You have allocated more than 7D. Reduce one or more skills until the remaining pool returns to 0D.
Builder.SpendAllDicePrompt: Spend the full 7D skill pool before creating the actor.
Builder.ForceCountsTowardPool: Force allocations come from the same 7D pool
Builder.NoPrintedForceSkills: This template does not have printed Force skills in the installed template data.

Errors.TemplateRequired: Select a template before creating a character.
Errors.ExactlySevenD: Allocate the full 7D skill pool before creating the actor.
Errors.Permission: You do not have permission to create actors.

Builder.ImportStartingEquipment: Import template starting equipment onto the created actor

Starship.RulesNote: The starship layer now includes combined roll helpers for speed, evasion, shields, gunnery, damage resolution, and the system-damage table.
Starship.OverviewHint: Starship Data from the core book is entered here.
Starship.CombatHint: Use the ship actions here for speed, evasion, shields, and manual system-damage rolls. Gunnery and damage resolution are on each weapon entry.
Starship.SystemsHint: Track damaged or disabled ship systems here, either in the core status fields or as individual ship-system items.
Starship.LoadoutHint: Mounted ship weapons, routes, and ship gear are listed here.

Helpers.OperatorPenaltyHint: Enter how many extra actions reduce the operator skill by -1D each. Ship codes are not reduced.
Helpers.SpeedSubtitle: Compare this total against the opposing ship's speed roll.
Helpers.SpeedCompareHint: Compare this total to the opposing ship's speed roll to see whether range closes, opens, or holds.
Helpers.EvasionSubtitle: Add this total to enemy gunnery difficulty for the current fire segment.
Helpers.EvasionApplyHint: Add this total to incoming attack difficulty for the current fire segment.
Helpers.ShieldSubtitle: A successful shield attempt protects against one incoming attack only.
Helpers.ShieldSuccessHint: Use this successful shield total for one incoming attack only, then compare damage against hull plus this shield roll.
Helpers.TorpedoHint: For torpedoes or missiles, add the target speed dice to the difficulty.
Helpers.TorpedoShortOnly: Torpedoes and missiles are short-range only.
Helpers.SystemDamageManual: The target has become severely damaged. Roll the Starship System Damage Table for the next effect.
Helpers.ShieldsBlownText: Light damage while shields were successfully used blows the shields and reduces future shield code by 1D until repaired.
Helpers.IonizedText: Light damage without successful shields ionizes the ship's controls.
Helpers.BlockedDestroyed: Destroyed ships cannot use this roll.
Helpers.BlockedDeadInSpace: Dead in space: this roll cannot be made.
Helpers.BlockedIonized: Ionized controls: this roll cannot be made until the ionization is cleared.
Helpers.BlockedIonDrives: Ion drives are disabled: no speed or maneuver rolls may be made.
Helpers.BlockedShieldSystem: Shields are disabled and cannot be rolled.
Helpers.BlockedNoCode: This code is below 1D and cannot currently be rolled.
Helpers.HeavyDamagePenalty: Heavy damage reduces this ship code by 1D until repaired.
Helpers.BlownShieldsPenalty: Blown shields reduce shields by a further 1D until repaired.
Helpers.LateralThrustersZero: Lateral thrusters disabled: maneuverability is reduced to 0D.
Helpers.MediumRangeDamagePenalty: Medium range reduces ship weapon damage by 1D.
Helpers.LongRangeDamagePenalty: Long range reduces ship weapon damage by 2D.
Helpers.NoTargetedStarship: Target exactly one opposing starship first.
Helpers.EngagementHint: Track short, medium, and long separately against each opposing ship.
Helpers.NoEngagements: No opposing ships are currently tracked here.
Helpers.RangeHeld: The ships hold their current range.
Helpers.LeftBattleArea: {target} is already at long range and breaks away from the battle area.
Helpers.TorpedoDissipated: Successful shields dissipate the torpedo or missile before it reaches the hull.
Helpers.HyperdriveDisabled: Hyperdrives are disabled and the ship cannot enter hyperspace.
Helpers.RepairHelperHint: Use this for starship repair checks and optionally update the ship state on a success.
Helpers.RepairApplied: Repair state updated on the ship.
Helpers.RepairNotApplied: Roll made only; the ship state was not changed.
Helpers.NothingToRepair: That target is not currently marked as damaged.
Helpers.StructuralDamageStepped: Damage state improves by one step.
Helpers.TemporaryStatusApplied: Temporary status applied to the target.
Helpers.SystemDamageAppliedStatus: Starship system damage also applied a temporary status to the target.

Astrogation.Title: {ship}: Astrogation Roll
Astrogation.Subtitle: Standard trips start at difficulty 15; adjust for pace, nav support, damage, and hasty entry.
Astrogation.InputHint: Use the route duration in days, then adjust for extra route time, the hyperdrive multiplier, and any faster or slower travel.
Astrogation.Failure: Astrogation fails and a mishap must be resolved.
Astrogation.HastyMishap: The ship enters hyperspace, but the rushed calculations cause a mishap.
Astrogation.NoEntryThisRound: The ship does not enter hyperspace this round.
Astrogation.MishapAuto: Triggered automatically because the astrogation roll failed after entry.
Astrogation.NoRecentSolution: No astrogation roll has been recorded on this ship yet.
Astrogation.HyperdriveDisabled: The ship cannot astrogate because its hyperdrives are disabled.

SW1E.NoShipItems: No items of this type yet.

Healing.ConsumeTrackedMedpac: Consume tracked Medpac from healer if available
Healing.IgnoreRestrictions: Ignore medpac restrictions for this treatment
Healing.IgnoreTiming: Complete rejuve treatment even if the timer has not expired
Healing.NoMedicineActor: No owned character or NPC with the Medicine skill is available.
Healing.NoMedicineSkill: {actor} does not have the Medicine skill.
Healing.NoMedpacStatus: Medpac treatment only applies to wounded, incapacitated, or mortally wounded characters.
Healing.NoNaturalStatus: Natural healing only applies to wounded or incapacitated characters.
Healing.NoRejuveStatus: Rejuve tank treatment only applies to wounded, incapacitated, or mortally wounded characters.
Healing.NoTankActive: {actor} is not currently in a rejuve tank.
Healing.TankNotReady: {actor} is still in a rejuve tank. Use the override to complete it early.
Healing.AlreadyTreated: {actor} has already been treated by medpac for this injury.
Healing.CooldownActive: {actor} cannot benefit from medpac again yet.
Healing.NaturalAlreadyRolled: {actor} has already made a natural healing roll in the last day.
Healing.NaturalNote: Use starting the day after injury; the helper only enforces the once-per-day roll.
Healing.RejuveAutomatic: {actor} heals automatically when the tank time ends.

Summary.RejuveReady: Rejuve tank treatment is ready to complete.
Summary.MedpacLocked: Medpac has already treated this injury.

Advancement.Title: Spend Skill Points — {actor}
Advancement.OnePipOnly: This helper spends one pip at a time.
Advancement.ManualNote: New non-Force skills, 0D skills, and the first Force-skill training step still need GM/manual handling.
Advancement.NoEligible: No eligible one-pip improvements were found on this actor.
Advancement.NotEnoughPoints: Not enough skill points. Cost: {cost}. Current pool: {points}.
Advancement.ForceAboveMaster: Double the cost for a Force skill above the master’s rating

ForcePoint.Title: Force Point
ForcePoint.NoneAvailable: This character has no Force Points remaining.
ForcePoint.AlreadyActive: A Force Point is already active for this actor.
ForcePoint.ResistanceModifier: Force Point: Strength code doubled for resistance
ForcePoint.StunIgnored: Force Point: stun ignored this round
ForcePoint.EffectSummary: All skill and attribute codes are doubled for this combat round. If the character is stunned this round, the stun is ignored.
ForcePoint.ChatCombat: The bonus is active for the current combat round and will drop when the next round begins.
ForcePoint.ChatManual: No active combat round was found, so the bonus stays active until you end it manually.
ForcePoint.ActiveCombat: Force Point active this combat round. Skills and attributes are doubled; stun is ignored this round.
ForcePoint.ActiveManual: Force Point active. Skills and attributes are doubled until you end it manually.
ForcePoint.Ended: The Force Point bonus has ended.

Combat.LightsaberParryNote: Sense is used for this lightsaber parry. The stored defense applies against melee attacks and blaster bolts this segment.

LightsaberParry.Title: Lightsaber Parry — {weapon}
LightsaberParry.NoLightsaber: This actor does not have a lightsaber available for Sense-based parries.
